title: 11 Herbal Creams For Inflamed Gums
intro_desc: Herbal creams for inflamed gums are a great alternative to chemical-based treatments. These creams typically contain soothing herbs that help calm down the inflammation and reduce pain. Aloe barbadensis, for example, is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote healing. It's also antibacterial, which means it can help prevent infections that can worsen gum inflammation.  Echinacea purpurea is another herb often used in herbal creams for inflamed gums. It's an immune system booster, which means it can help your body fight off infections and reduce inflammation. Some studies have shown that Echinacea can even reduce the severity and duration of gum inflammation.  Curcuma longa, or turmeric, is also a popular ingredient in herbal creams for inflamed gums. It contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Curcumin can help reduce inflammation and prevent infections, making it an effective treatment for gum inflammation.  Using herbal creams for inflamed gums can bring several benefits to your life. For one, they're a natural alternative to chemical-based treatments, which can be harsh on your skin and gums. They're also often free of harsh chemicals and additives, making them a safer choice for people with sensitive skin or allergies. Additionally, herbal creams can be used in conjunction with other natural remedies, such as oil pulling and mouth rinses, to create a comprehensive oral health routine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a high amount of aloin and aloe-emodin, which are powerful anti-inflammatory compounds. These components help reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissue, making Aloe barbadensis creams effective for inflamed gums. Aloe-emodin also has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infection and promote healing in inflamed gum tissue. Aloin and other bioactive compounds in Aloe barbadensis creams also possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect gums from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Aloe barbadensis creams can help promote healthy gum tissue and alleviate inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 10 drops of tea tree oil, and 10 drops of peppermint oil.
In a small saucepan, heat the distilled water over low heat. Add the aloe vera gel and stir until dissolved.
In a separate bowl, mix the coconut oil and beeswax. Heat the mixture in the microwave for 10-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until melted.
Combine the aloe vera mixture and the coconut oil mixture. Stir in the tea tree oil and peppermint oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once cooled and thickened, your aloe barbadensis cream is ready to use. Store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and flavonoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria that can cause gum infections. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams help to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, while the caffeic acid derivatives have antioxidant properties that help to protect the gums from oxidative damage. The flavonoids in the cream have antimicrobial properties that help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and inflammation. By using Echinacea purpurea creams, you can reduce inflammation and promote healing in your gums, helping to prevent further complications.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the distilled water to the melted mixture and stir well. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool and thicken for about 30 minutes. Then, add the dried Echinacea purpurea root and stir well.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Let it cool and solidify before applying to inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory. Curcumin has potent antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the gums from damage. Curcuma longa creams also contain a compound called demethoxycurcumin, which has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes, reducing swelling and pain in inflamed gums. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcuma longa creams also come from its ability to suppress the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that promote inflammation. The combination of curcumin and demethoxycurcumin in Curcuma longa creams makes it an effective treatment for inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Melt 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of turmeric powder and 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the melted coconut oil.
Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved, then remove from heat.
Add 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to the mixture. Stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol. These bioactive constituents have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat gum inflammation. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums. Terpinen-4-ol has been found to have a strong antibacterial effect, which helps to prevent gum infections that can cause inflammation. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help to soothe and heal inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of water, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 10 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Melt beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat. Add coconut oil and shea butter. Stir until melted and smooth.
Remove saucepan from heat. Stir in water until well combined.
Add Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and stir well. Pour mixture into a clean, sterilized container.
Allow mixture to cool and harden. Once hardened, your Melaleuca alternifolia cream is ready to use for inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause inflammation in the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of gingerol and shogaol help to reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums, making it easier to manage conditions like gingivitis and periodontitis. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Zingiber officinale creams, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to neutralize free radicals that can cause tissue damage and exacerbate inflammation in the gums. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Zingiber officinale creams can help to soothe and protect inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the Zingiber officinale powder and stir well to combine. Remove from heat.
Mix in the vitamin E oil and aloe vera gel. Stir until smooth and well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let cool until solid. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains compounds like eugenol and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums. The camphor in eucalyptus globulus creams has a numbing effect that can help soothe sore gums. Eucalyptol, another key component, has a cooling effect that may help decrease inflammation and ease discomfort. By applying eucalyptus globulus creams to inflamed gums, these active constituents work together to provide relief from swelling and pain.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the coconut oil.
Add 10 drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the mixture.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter melt. Stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool. Pour into a container and store in the fridge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains active constituents like tannins, ursolic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. Tannins, in particular, have been shown to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Rosmarinic acid, on the other hand, has antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. By using Salvia officinalis creams, individuals can potentially alleviate inflamed gums by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves to the melted mixture and steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the strained mixture with vitamin E oil and aloe vera gel. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and harden. Your Salvia officinalis cream is now ready to use. Apply a small amount to inflamed gums as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are the active constituents responsible for its therapeutic properties. Flavonoids, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums. Terpenoids, including ginkgolides and bilobalide, have antioxidant properties that protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba help to soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing pain and discomfort. Ginkgo biloba's unique combination of flavonoids and terpenoids makes it a valuable natural remedy for treating inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of Ginkgo biloba essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in the Ginkgo biloba essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool and harden before using it to treat inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins and sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe inflamed gums. The saponins in Calendula officinalis inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing swelling and redness in the gums. The sesquiterpene lactones in Calendula officinalis also have antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections that can cause gum inflammation. Additionally, Calendula officinalis creams contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the gums. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Calendula officinalis creams can help alleviate gum inflammation and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and a double boiler.
Dry and prepare the Calendula flowers by spreading them on a baking sheet and letting them air dry for 1 week. Store them in an airtight container.
Combine the dried Calendula flowers and coconut oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into the double boiler. Add beeswax and heat the mixture until the beeswax melts. Stir in vitamin E oil.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it solidify. Use the cream as needed to soothe inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active constituents that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Thymol is effective against a wide range of bacteria that can cause gum infections, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent further infection. Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums. Thymus vulgaris creams also contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, Thymus vulgaris creams can help to promote healing and reduce symptoms of inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of water, 1/2 cup of dried Thymus vulgaris, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine water and Thymus vulgaris in a saucepan and heat on low for 10 minutes to make a tea.
Strain the tea and let it cool. Mix the cooled tea with coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin E oil in a separate bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating, until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and harden. Use the cream as needed to soothe inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation and pain. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams also have antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections that can cause inflammation in gums. When applied topically, these compounds can help soothe and calm inflamed gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup distilled water, 1/2 cup glycerin, 2 tablespoons glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 1 teaspoon aloe vera gel, and 1 teaspoon vitamin E oil.
Combine distilled water and glycerin in a saucepan. Heat on low until glycerin dissolves.
Remove saucepan from heat and add glycyrrhiza glabra root powder. Stir until powder dissolves.
Let mixture cool. Add aloe vera gel and vitamin E oil. Mix well.
Pour mixture into a clean container. Store in the refrigerator. Use as a topical cream to soothe inflamed gums.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent inflamed gums from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the gums. The antioxidants in these teas may also help reduce swelling and promote overall oral health. Regularly drinking herbal tea may be beneficial in maintaining healthy gums.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good option to help with inflamed gums. However, it's essential to note that some herbs can be too strong if consumed daily. For example, peppermint tea can irritate gums if had in excess. It's best to drink herbal teas in moderation, like one cup a day, to see how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce inflamed gums, but it takes time to see results. It may start to work within a few days to a week, but noticeable improvement often takes 2-4 weeks. You might notice a slight reduction in swelling and pain, and your gums may start to look healthier.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for inflamed gums is best done in the morning. This allows you to start the day with a soothing effect that can last for several hours. The anti-inflammatory properties of the tea can help calm swollen gums, making it easier to eat and speak throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with some prescription medications for inflamed gums. For example, if you're taking blood thinners, certain teas like ginkgo biloba or garlic tea might increase their effects and cause bleeding. Also, teas like echinacea or licorice root may affect how your body reacts to antibiotics.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have inflamed gums. Some herbs might not be safe for your baby. For example, sage and chamomile can stimulate the uterus, which isn't good during pregnancy. If you still want to try herbal teas, look for ones that are specifically labeled as pregnancy-safe.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to calm inflamed gums, but be cautious when breastfeeding. Some herbs like sage, peppermint, and chamomile are generally considered safe, but it's best to check the ingredients and avoid strong or concentrated brews. If your baby seems affected, try a different tea or reduce the amount consumed.
title_2: Smokers Beware: 11 Herbal Creams for Inflamed Gums
